description_long: This is a two-story residential house with a symmetrical front facade, featuring a covered porch supported by white columns and brick steps leading up to the entryway. The exterior is clad in light gray horizontal siding with white trim around the windows and roofline, and the roof is a dark gray gable style with two prominent red brick chimneys. The front elevation displays three upper windows and two lower windows flanking the central door, with decorative shingles and patterned brickwork adding visual interest. Both sides of the house have evenly spaced windows, and the porch wraps partially around the front corners. The foundation is constructed of red brick, and the porch includes white railings and latticework. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century American homes, blending functional simplicity with modest decorative details.
description: A two-story gray house with a gable roof, brick foundation, and a covered front porch with white columns and railings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the symmetrical facade, covered porch with white columns, central entry door, and three upper windows.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the horizontal siding, two sets of double windows, and the side extension of the porch.
description_view_2: Rear view with a simpler facade, two upper windows, a central door, and brick steps, lacking the decorative front details.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, with two sets of double windows and visible porch extension.
attribute_entryway_design: Central door with brick steps, covered porch supported by white columns, white railings, and decorative latticework
